title: Quintinia media
intro: Quintinia media, also known as the prickly quartz tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Quintinia media include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Quintinia media include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Quintinia media is classified as a member of the family Cephalotaceae, a family of flowering plants native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is also known as the tuckeroo or wheel tree. The Cephalotaceae family is part of the larger order of Sapindales, a group of plants that includes many species of trees and shrubs. Quintinia media is a small to medium-sized tree that is often found in coastal areas and woodlands. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
